name: Whitefly
damage: Sucks phloem sap causing leaf yellowing and wilting
Excretes honeydew promoting sooty mold growth
PRIMARY VECTOR for geminiviruses (Leaf Curl, Yellow Mosaic)
Severe infestations cause leaf drop and stunting
identification: Tiny (1-2mm) white moth-like insects on leaf undersides. Fly up in clouds when disturbed.

name: Helicoverpa armigera (Fruit Borer / Pod Borer)
damage: Larvae bore into fruits, pods, and bolls from inside
Single larva can destroy 8-10 tomato fruits
Causes 30-40% yield loss in chickpea and pigeon pea
Difficult to control once inside the fruit
identification: Moth: yellowish-brown with dark spots on forewings. Larva: green/brown with dark stripes, up to 4cm.
economic_threshold_level: 1-2 larvae per plant (tomato); 2-3 per meter row (chickpea)

biological: HaNPV (Helicoverpa Nuclear Polyhedrosis Virus) — 250 LE/acre at dusk

name: Brown Plant Hopper (BPH)
damage: Sucks phloem at stem base causing 'hopper burn' — circular patches of dried plants
Excretes honeydew causing sooty mold
Transmits grassy stunt and ragged stunt viruses
Can cause complete crop loss in severe outbreaks
identification: Brown 3-4mm insects at base of rice tillers near water level. Can jump/fly.

biological: Conserve spiders (Lycosa, Tetragnatha) — avoid broad-spectrum sprays

name: Fall Armyworm
damage: Larvae feed voraciously on leaves — windowpane effect then ragged holes
Bore into growing point (whorl) of maize causing dead hearts
Massive frass visible in whorl
Newly invasive in India (since 2018) — spreading rapidly across states
identification: Larva: brown/green with inverted Y mark on head, 4 dark spots in square pattern on last segment. Moth: grey-brown.

cultural: Destroy crop residue immediately after harvest
biological: Bt spray at early larval stage

name: Aphids
damage: Suck sap from tender shoots, leaves, and flowers
Cause leaf curling, yellowing, and stunting
Secrete honeydew leading to sooty mold
Vector for numerous plant viruses (Potato Leaf Roll, CMV)
identification: Soft-bodied, pear-shaped, 1-3mm. Green, yellow, or black colonies on growing tips and leaf undersides.
economic_threshold_level: 25-30 aphids per plant (mustard); 50 per ear head (wheat)

cultural: Early sowing to escape peak populations
biological: Lady beetles (Coccinella septempunctata) — most effective predator
organic: Strong water jet to dislodge colonies

name: Pink Bollworm
damage: Larvae bore into green bolls and feed on lint and seeds
Rosette flower (stuck petals) is diagnostic symptom
Causes 20-60% yield loss in late-season cotton
Has developed resistance to Cry1Ac protein in Bt cotton in several regions
identification: Moth: small brown (7-8mm). Larva: pink with brown head, found inside bolls.
economic_threshold_level: 8-10% boll damage or 8 moths/trap/week in pheromone traps

chemical: Profenophos 50% EC (2ml/L) — rotate with other chemistries
